stories.title: Russian drone attacks persist despite Kremlin’s Easter ceasefire, Ukrainian forces say
stories.summary: Ukraine reports 469 violations of Putin’s 32-hour ceasefire, hours after deadly drone attacks on Odesa and Kherson Russia continued to strike Ukrainian positions with drones after a Kremlin-declared Easter ceasefire took effect on Saturday, a Ukrainian military officer said. “The ceasefire is not being observed by the Russian side,” said Serhii Kolesnychenko, a communications officer for the 148th Separate Artillery Brigade. Continue reading...

stories.title: Is this the end of Viktor Orbán’s regime? - podcast
stories.summary: “Hungary has been a model for the Trump presidency for a while now,” the Guardian journalist Flora Garamvolgyi tells Helen Pidd. “And US Republicans looked at Hungary for these past years as a model to follow. “[Viktor] Orbán is currently on his fourth consecutive term. And the fact that he has been so successful and he had similar narrative, similar ideologies to US Republicans in terms of immigration, for example, I think they have found a link to connect with Orbán and they were studying his

stories.title: Explosives found near pipeline in Serbia probably ‘Russian provocation’, says expert
stories.summary: Former Ukrainian major general says 4kg of material was most likely an attempt to influence Hungary’s election Hungary elections explainer The amount of explosives discovered in Serbia last week would not have been enough to destroy the Balkan Stream gas pipeline, prompting an expert to conclude it was probably a Russian intelligence plot aimed at influencing Hungary’s impending election. A former Ukrainian major general and a munitions specialist told the Guardian calculations made by his compa

stories.title: Key Democrats call on Eric Swalwell to end campaign for California governor
stories.summary: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries and former Speaker Nancy Pelosi headlined a growing list of Democratic lawmakers called on Rep. Eric Swalwell Friday to withdraw his campaign for California governor amid  allegations of sexual misconduct . “This extremely sensitive matter must be appropriately investigated with full transparency and accountability,” Pelosi said in a statement. “As I discussed with Congressman Swalwell, it is clear that is best done outside of a gubernatorial campaign.”

stories.title: Hungarians vote in hard-fought election that could oust Viktor Orbán after 16 years
stories.summary: Rightwing leader trails in polls to Péter Magyar, despite support from JD Vance on recent visit Hungarians are heading to the ballot boxes to vote in a hard-fought parliamentary election that could oust Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power and potentially reshape the central European country’s relations with the EU, Moscow and Washington. In the campaign, Orbán – the EU’s longest-serving leader – has trailed in the polls as he faces an unprecedented challenge from Péter Magyar, a former elite me

stories.title: How Hungary's knife-edge election could impact the US and Russia
stories.summary: The BBC's Europe Editor Katya Adler reports from Prime Minister Victor Orbán's home town of Felscút.

stories.title: ‘He cares about Hungarians’: the small Ukrainian town divided over Orbán
stories.summary: The rightwing populist’s support for the majority-Hungarian population of Berehove means they may offer their votes in return Hungary elections: what is at stake and who is likely to win? Across much of Ukraine, Sunday’s parliamentary election in Hungary is being followed with a singular hope: that Viktor Orbán, the Kremlin-friendly leader who has made opposition to Kyiv a centrepiece of his campaign, will be voted out after 16 years in office. But in Berehove, the mood is more complicated. Cont

stories.title: How much sway does Trump have over Hungarian voters?
stories.summary: US President Trump has backed Hungarian PM Orban. It's unclear whether the endorsement will help in Hungary's election — the European hard-right has struggled with Trump's recent anti-Europe policies and threats.

stories.title: Hungary election: Orban faces tight race in pivotal vote
stories.summary: Hungarians are heading to the polls in an election that could end Prime Minister Viktor Orban's 16-year rule. Opposition leader Peter Magyar has emerged as the biggest threat to Orban's grip on power.

stories.title: Hungarians decide whether to end 16 years of Orbán rule and elect rival
stories.summary: Most polls favour Péter Magyar, who fronts a grassroots party, but PM Viktor Orbán has been in a defiant mood.

stories.title: Human rights groups decry US plan for Guantánamo camp for Cuban migrants
stories.summary: Exclusive: Dozens of organizations write to Congress after general announced plan to ‘deal with’ those fleeing any humanitarian crisis on the island Dozens of US and international human rights organizations are decrying the Trump administration ’s plans to establish a migrant “camp” for fleeing Cubans at the Guantánamo Bay military base if the island nation’s crisis worsens under pressure from the US, according to a letter to members of Congress on Friday. The 85 groups plan to submit the joint

stories.title: Hungarians speak to the Guardian before decisive election – video
stories.summary: Voters will head to the polls on Sunday in what is seen as Europe’s most consequential election of the year. The Guardian's Jakub Krupa and Flora Garamvolgyi speak to Hungarians in Budapest before the vote Continue reading...

stories.title: 1,6 millióra bírságolták Márki-Zayt a videó miatt, amiben válogatott szidalmakkal illette a csádi missziót támogató szavazókat
stories.summary: A választási bizottság szerint a hódmezővásárhelyi polgármester jogtalanul használja kampányolásra a hivatalos Facebook-csatornáját.

stories.title: Ezek voltak a legfontosabb üzenetek Orbán Viktor és Magyar Péter kampányzáróin
stories.summary: A Tisza elnöke azt mondta, hogy „holnap visszavesszük ezt a gyönyörű országot”, Orbán Viktor pedig arról beszélt, hogy az volt a feladatuk, hogy ők legyenek a hangos többség és ez sikerült is.

stories.title: „Először vagyok fideszes rendezvényen, de megfélemlítve érzem magam”
stories.summary: Sokan voltak a fideszes kampányzárón a Várnegyedben, de nem mindenki azért ment, hogy Orbánt hallgassa. A fideszes országjárást végigkísérő csapat tagjai jól kitűntek a tömegből.

stories.title: A lengyel hírszerzés volt vezetője szerint nevén kell nevezni a dolgokat, Orbán és Szijjártó orosz ügynökök
stories.summary: Andrzej Derlatka azt sem teljesen érti, Szijjártó miért jár annyit Belaruszba.

stories.title: Aleksandar Vučić Orbánnak: Még ha veszítesz is, örökké hálásak leszünk
stories.summary: A szerb elnök mellett Robert Fico, Szlovákia miniszterelnöke is üzent Orbán Viktornak. Azt kívánta neki, „a parlamenti választásokon teljesüljön minden politikai célja”.

stories.title: Akkor vége van
stories.summary: De csak a kampánynak. Vasárnap az eddig legintenzívebb választási időszak után Magyarország választ. A korábbi kampányok után újdonság volt, hogy a legfontosabb események az utcákon és a tereken zajlottak, és hogy Magyar Péter után most a magát „street fighterként” azonosító Orbán Viktor is utcára ment. Fotósaink legjobb felvételeiből válogattunk.

stories.title: Magyar részvételi rekordot vár, Orbán szerint „minél többen vagyunk, annál jobb”
stories.summary: Reggel 7 óráig a választópolgárok 3,46 százaléka ment el szavazni, ez hatalmas rekord. Magyar Péter is leadta szavazatát. Kövesse a választás napját a Telexen percről percre.

stories.title: Volt már olyan szavazás április 12-én, amikor élesen bírálták Orbán Viktort
stories.summary: Brüsszel és Moszkva már 23 éve is elhangzott egy mondatban, amikor Magyarország európai uniós csatlakozásáról tartottak ügydöntő népszavazást.

stories.title: Az egész világ a magyar választásra figyel
stories.summary: A külföldi lapok az egész jobboldali-populista mozgalom szempontjából sorsszerűként jellemzik a magyar választást, mindenki nemzetközi szinten is messzemenő következtetéséket vonna le az eredményből.

stories.title: Orbán: Nagy vereség kellene ahhoz, hogy lemondjak – egész napos választási élő
stories.summary: Egész nap helyszíni bejelentkezésekkel, interjúkkal, elemzésekkel, térképekkel mutatjuk meg, mi történik a választás napján. Stúdiónkban Bódi Mátyás, hamarosan pedig élőben szavaz Orbán Viktor és Magyar Péter.

stories.title: Voting begins in Hungary with record turnout in first hour of high-stakes election
stories.summary: If the race ends up being too close to call, the final result may not be known for several days after polls close.

stories.title: An environmental disaster in Moldova has Russia’s fingerprints all over it | Paula Erizanu
stories.summary: The Ukraine war on our doorstep is a constant threat. Contaminated drinking water is a dangerous new twist In the second week of March, the nature vlogger Ilie Cojocari went out to film the arrival of spring on the Nistru (Dniester) river, 70 metres away from his home in Naslavcea, a village bordering Ukraine on the northernmost point of Moldova. But as he approached the river he could smell the stench of oil rising up from the water and see dark spots floating on its surface. Something was wron

stories.title: As Hungary gears up for a historic election, DW answers a few key questions
stories.summary: Hungary's election on April 12 is the most important for the state since 1989, and also of historic significance for the EU. DW takes a more detailed look.

stories.title: Hungary: Scandal, fear and hope ahead of vote
stories.summary: Fake news and a growing number of political scandals: Hungary hasn't seen an election this fraught since after 1989's collapse of the Soviet Union.

stories.title: Hungary Election: An Orbán victory would be good news for Europe
stories.summary: Hungary elections: An Orbán victory would be good news for Europe, writes Gerolf Annemans of the “Patriots for Europe” group in the European Parliament in an op-ed for Euronews. Annemans says Orbán has become a symbol of resistance to centralisation.

stories.title: „már készülünk is a rendszerváltásra, de nem tudjuk előre, bőrönd lesz-e belőle vagy válltáska”
stories.summary: Közéleti versek a Telexen! Három magyar szerzőt kértünk fel arra, hogy a költészet napja alkalmából írjanak verset a hazai közéletre reagálva. Harmadikként Háy János alkotását közöljük.

stories.title: Addig rajzolgatták a választási térképet, amíg sikerült egy hatfős budai szavazókört alkotni
stories.summary: A Nemzeti Választási Iroda szerint a Budaörsi út melletti szavazóficak „még ha furcsának is tűnik, így törvényes”.

stories.title: Melania Trump váratlanul ismét felhozta az Epstein-ügyet, ami az elnöknek sem jön jól
stories.summary: Megy a találgatás arról, hogy miért épp most szólalt meg a first lady a botrányról, amely miatt korábban az elnöknek már magyarázkodnia kellett.

stories.title: „Kérem, telepítsen magyar fegyveres erőket szuverén területünkre” – itt van a csádi elnök Orbánnak küldött 2023-as levele
stories.summary: Akár fegyveres terrorista csoportokkal szembeni közvetlen küzdelemre is bevetette volna a magyar katonákat a csádi elnök, ha megvalósul a honvédség csádi missziója. Orbánnak küldött levele szerint „a magyar fegyveres erőknek jogukban fog állni erőt alkalmazni, beleértve szükség esetén a halálos erőt is.”

stories.title: Magyar Péter lényegében győzelmi beszédet tartott, Orbán nehéz időkre számít – kampányt zártak a nagypártok
stories.summary: Orbán akkora győzelemre számít, amellyel magukat is meglepik, Magyar szerint vasárnap megerősödik, hogy Európában van a helyünk. Élőben közvetítettük kettejük kampányzáróját, a stúdióban pedig Bódi Mátyással elemeztünk.

stories.title: Sérültet mentett a barlangi mentős a Mátyás-hegyen, hazafelé tartott, amikor előtte zuhant le az autó a Göncz Árpád városközpont felüljárójáról
stories.summary: A Magyar Barlangi Mentőszolgálat riasztásvezetője a Mátyás-hegyi-barlangból tartott haza, amikor közvetlenül előtte zuhant le az autó a felüljáróról. A mentők és a tűzoltók kiérkezéséig megkezdte a súlyos sérült újraélesztését.

stories.title: Hogyan lettek „élet-halál urai” a fideszes képviselők a területfejlesztésben?
stories.summary: A központosítás nyomán az országgyűlési képviselők vették át a területfejlesztés irányítását az önkormányzatoktól az elmúlt évtizedben, ami növelte a politikai befolyást és a korrupciós kockázatokat.

stories.title: Dúró Dóra szerint minden eddiginél nagyobb esély van arra, hogy a nemzeti radikalizmus megkerülhetetlen tényező legyen a parlamentben
stories.summary: Budapesten zárta szombaton a kampányát a Mi Hazánk Mozgalom.

stories.title: Political Capital: Magyarország helye a világban hosszú időre eldőlhet
stories.summary: Az elemzőintézet szerint ha az Orbán-rezsim kormányon marad, belátható időn belül búcsút inthet az ország az Európai Uniónak és a nyugati szövetségi rendszernek.

stories.title: Poland: "Frog Patrol" saves thousands of amphibians during migration
stories.summary: Volunteers near Warsaw form a frog patrol each spring, helping thousands of frogs and toads cross a road safely and protect local breeding grounds.

stories.title: Elections in Hungary: What do the polls say?
stories.summary: Medián, currently considered among the most accurate pollsters in Hungary, predicts a two-thirds majority in parliament for opposition party Tisza. Most other pollsters estimate a smaller lead for them, while few have the ruling Fidesz party narrowly ahead.

stories.title: Hungary Elections: There is hope that Peter Magyar can deliver a new beginning
stories.summary: What will Hungary’s elections bring? In an op-ed for Euronews, EU politician Valérie Hayer expresses hope that Péter Magyar could usher in a new political beginning, while warning that another term for Viktor Orbán risks cementing irreversible democratic backsliding.

stories.title: Even if Victor Orbán is ousted on Sunday, Hungary’s return to liberal democracy is not guaranteed | Gabriela Greilinger and Cas Mudde
stories.summary: Challenger Péter Magyar is no progressive – and after 16 years of creeping authoritarianism, the PM has embedded Fidesz in the Hungarian state On Sunday, Hungarians will go to the polls to decide on their country’s direction for the next four years in an election that looks as if it will be a nail-biter. Viktor Orbán, Europe’s longest-serving prime minister – who has been in power for 16 years and transformed his country into an electoral autocracy – could lose the election . Ahead of the vote,

stories.title: Fact check: Orban points to Ukraine over pipeline bomb
stories.summary: Orban strongly suggests explosives found near a key gas pipeline in Serbia are evidence of Ukrainian sabotage. Serbia's intelligence chief publicly disagrees.

stories.title: Hungary election: Voters want new EU approach, but tough Ukraine stance, poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll ahead of Hungary's election suggests that most voters want at least minor changes to ties with the EU. But it also found skepticism of financial support for Ukraine, and voters prioritizing domestic issues.

stories.title: A new generation of politicians of colour is emerging in France. The backlash speaks volumes
stories.summary: Local elections have led to a surge of racism in a country that still struggles to see itself as anything other than white Saint-Denis is just over 9km from the centre of Paris but is in the poorest department in all of metropolitan France, a region marked by unemployment, low incomes and social disadvantage. But Saint Denis’s town hall was the backdrop to memorably joyous celebrations on the evening of 15 March. A delirious crowd carried the new mayor shoulder high, chanting his name over and o

stories.title: Virtual Romanian singer goes viral but sparks criticism
stories.summary: In Romania, an AI-generated singer inspired by poems about the Roma community has become an overnight success. But critics say her creator is monetizing the woes of a marginalized group without involving its members.

stories.title: Hungarians living in Ukraine caught between front lines
stories.summary: About 80,000 ethnic Hungarians live in western Ukraine. Many feel torn between Viktor Orban's anti-Ukrainian campaign and the conflict between the two countries. What does the future hold for this minority?

stories.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

stories.title: Bunker busters and a Burger King: a visual guide to US military bases on British soil
stories.summary: War with Iran has brought 15 American sites across the UK countryside firmly into the spotlight They are dotted across the UK countryside, often obscured from public view behind highly secured perimeter fences. Technically, they are on British soil, and misleadingly most have “Royal Air Force” in their name. But in many respects, these military outposts are under the control of the US president and commander-in-chief. Continue reading...

stories.title: US and Iran fail to reach agreement after marathon talks
stories.summary: U.S. Vice President JD Vance and other top Trump administration officials are heading home empty handed. Marathon negotiations with Iran in Pakistan this weekend failed to produce a breakthrough that would definitively end the war, which is entering its seventh week. Vance, who spoke early Sunday morning local time, said the Iranians refused to give assurances that they would not try to obtain or develop a nuclear weapon. “They have chosen not to accept our terms,” Vance said. “The simple fact i

stories.title: Trump endorses ‘focused’ immigration enforcement funding bill
stories.summary: President Donald Trump gave his blessing Friday afternoon for a party-line package focused narrowly on immigration enforcement — in a boost to Senate GOP leaders amid the Department of Homeland Security funding stalemate. Trump’s comments came after he met Friday with Senate Budget Chair Lindsey Graham of South Carolina and Senate Majority Whip John Barrasso of Wyoming. The two lawmakers went to the White House to pitch Senate GOP leadership’s plan to restrict the party’s fil

stories.title: UK puts Diego Garcia handover on hold to appease Trump
stories.summary: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s government is setting aside a plan to hand over the Chagos Islands to Mauritius because of opposition from U.S. President Donald Trump’s administration, according to British media reports.  A bill underpinning the deal will not be included in the King’s Speech next month, The Times and other outlets reported on Saturday. POLITICO reported in February that Starmer’s administration would “pause for thought” on the Chagos plan. The biggest island

stories.title: Susan was forced out of a disability support job after speaking out. Are NDIS whistleblower laws still too weak?
stories.summary: Human rights lawyers say NDIS workers and their clients remain at risk despite newly bolstered whistleblower protections Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast When Susan* came across wrongdoing at her disability support provider, she faced a choice. Say nothing, and allow her highly vulnerable clients to be put at serious risk. Continue reading...

stories.title: New relief for households being considered as Albanese government warns of ‘long tail’ from Iran war
stories.summary: Catherine King says while peace talks were ‘best chance’ at lowering fuel prices, further help may be included in budget Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Track Australia’s fuel prices, service station outages and shipments in charts Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Albanese government is contemplating further relief for struggling households and businesses in next month’s federal budget, as peace talks continue between the US and Iran amid a

stories.title: Trump reportedly says he’ll issue mass pardons at end of his presidential term
stories.summary: President already has issued sweeping pardons throughout second term, including for 1,500 US Capital riot defendants Donald Trump has reportedly said he will issue pardons en masse to his closest advisers at the end of his second presidency, promising them in casual conversations over the last year. “I’ll pardon everyone who has come within 200 feet of the Oval [Office],” the president reportedly said in a recent meeting, garnering laughs from the room, according to a Wall Street Journal report

stories.title: Suspect in New York subway machete attack shot and killed by police
stories.summary: Three people wounded in attack at Grand Central subway station by man who stated he was ‘Lucifer’, police said Police in New York City shot and killed a man who stabbed three people on a subway platform in New York City ’s Grand Central station, the city’s police commissioner said. Jessica Tisch, who leads the New York police department, told reporters at a news conference at the station that officers, flagged down by a witness to the stabbings at about 9.40am, had encountered a suspect, armed w

stories.title: French man charged with keeping nine-year-old son locked in van since 2024
stories.summary: Police rescued boy after neighbour reported sounds of a child coming from vehicle in Hagenbach in eastern France A malnourished nine-year-old boy was rescued after being locked in his father’s van since 2024 in eastern France, a prosecutor said. A neighbour alerted police to “sounds of a child” coming from a vehicle in the village of Hagenbach, near the borders of Switzerland and Germany. Continue reading...

stories.title: UFOs: 'Most are reported by people out for an evening smoke'
stories.summary: Hansjürgen Köhler has been a volunteer UFO hunter for more than 50 years. He sees his work as scientific investigation, but sometimes also as caring for others.

stories.title: Irish cabinet meeting to be held to discuss ongoing fuel protests
stories.summary: Hundreds of petrol stations in the Republic of Ireland have run out of fuel as the protests and blockades continued for a fifth day.

stories.title: Why this disillusioned Trump voter spends hours searching Epstein files
stories.summary: Many who support the president have expressed frustration with how Trump's former attorney general Pam Bondi handled the Epstein files.

stories.title: L’Arc de Trump: Plans unveiled for 250-foot arch in Washington
stories.summary: The Trump administration Friday unveiled new renderings of its proposed triumphal arch, a 250-foot structure planned to tower over a traffic circle near Arlington National Cemetery. The memorial, dubbed “Independence Arch,” is part of President Donald Trump’s broader effort to leave a mark on the nation’s capitol and to commemorate the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence this year. “The Triumphal Arch in Memorial Circle is going to be one of the most iconic landma

stories.title: Ukraine and Russia swap 175 war prisoners as Easter ceasefire starts
stories.summary: Ukraine and Russia swapped 175 prisoners of war each on Saturday as a 32-hour ceasefire began on the occasion of Orthodox Easter. Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy posted pictures of the Ukrainian soldiers being welcomed back home. The group includes troops who have fought on different war fronts, from Mariupol to the Donetsk, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia regions. Seven civilians were included. “Some of them are wounded. Most have been in captivity since 2022. And finally — they are home,” Zel

stories.title: US and Iran hold talks in Islamabad as Pakistan seeks to broker peace deal
stories.summary: JD Vance leads American delegation while Iran’s negotiators headed up by Iran’s parliamentary speaker Peace talks between Iran and the US began in Islamabad this afternoon, with senior negotiators from both countries meeting face to face at the highest level for the first time since 1979, in the presence of mediators from Pakistan . Pakistani state TV said US and Iranian officials were “sitting directly at the same table” – which was later confirmed by the White House – and discussions were begi

stories.title: Washington officials say 21 dead canines that washed ashore were foxes, solving local mystery
stories.summary: Animals were used legally as fishing bait, sheriff’s office confirms after incident shook locals in Guemes Island A Washington state sheriff’s office says it has solved the mystery of nearly two dozen dead canines who washed ashore recently . The animals were foxes being used legally as bait for fishing operations, the Skagit county sheriff’s office said on Friday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Islamabad negotiations end without a deal – as it happened
stories.summary: US vice-president says Iran chose not to accept American terms for a deal, including to not build nuclear weapons JD Vance says US delegation leaving Pakistan after failing to reach deal with Iran US officials claim Iran unable to find mines it laid in strait of Hormuz The UK will host a strait of Hormuz meeting next week , bringing together multiple countries aiming to restore free movement of ships through the strait, which has been blockaded by Iran since the beginning of the war and inflicte

stories.title: Money to burn? The humble matchbox gets a £235 makeover
stories.summary: Described as the ‘must-have’ home accessory of 2026, sales of ‘posh’ matchboxes are up 121% at Selfridges Goodbye Swan Vesta, hello Cartier. Matchboxes are the latest home accessory to get a luxury makeover – and some of the price tags are striking. At the upmarket department store chain Selfridges, sales of posh matchboxes are up 121% year-on-year and it said they were “the must-have home accessory for 2026”. The store has more than doubled its range to meet demand, selling over 100 styles at p

stories.title: Low-tax Texas opens London office to lure jobs and investment
stories.summary: Exclusive: US state is targeting corporate heavyweights in the UK with subsidies and incentives Y’all Street: Bullish Dallas aims to steal New York’s crown The US state of Texas is putting UK businesses in its crosshairs with the launch this month of a dedicated London office to lure jobs and investment to the low-tax Lone Star State. Texas recently secured approval for the new site, adding to a growing list of international offices from which it can try to draw corporate heavyweights across its

stories.title: ‘Another institutional abuse’: UK survivor of Irish mother and baby home can’t afford to accept compensation
stories.summary: Rosemary Adaser, 70, would lose £1,000 a month in benefits, while her twin brother in Ireland got full amount with no penalty A twin sister and brother were separated from their parents in Ireland’s mother and baby home scandal – but only one of them can be compensated without being penalised. Rosemary Adaser, 70, of Ealing, west London, was among tens of thousands of children placed in abusive institutions for being born out of marriage in Ireland. Continue reading...

stories.title: Chalmers warns of ‘more polarising politics’ – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Penny Wong calls failed peace talks between US and Iran ‘disappointing’ and urges resumption Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Seven-year-old girl drowns at swimming spot on Brisbane River A seven-year-old girl has drowned at a popular swimming spot on the Brisbane River in the south-west of the city, AAP reports. Continue reading...

stories.title: Penny Wong calls failed peace talks between US and Iran ‘disappointing’ and urges resumption
stories.summary: Australia’s foreign affairs minister says priority ‘must be to continue the ceasefire and return to negotiations’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s foreign affairs minister, Penny Wong, has urged the US and Iran to continue the ceasefire and return to negotiations quickly, after peace talks failed to secure a deal or the re-opening of the strait of Hormuz. Historic face-to-face meetings in Pakistan – marking the highest-level of direct engagement between Wa

stories.title: ‘Your photos will be deleted’: Apple users warned over ‘nasty’ iCloud storage scam
stories.summary: Fraudsters send emails claiming storage is full or nearly full, then trick people into clicking on links that can expose bank and personal details For a while you’ve been getting messages from Apple saying “your iCloud storage is full”. They say you have exceeded your storage plan, so documents are no longer being backed up, and photos you take aren’t being uploaded. You have been resisting Apple’s efforts to get you to pay a minimum of 99p a month for more storage. But it seems that you can’t k

stories.title: ‘We’re trapped’: despair for sellers as Iran war knocks confidence in UK housing market
stories.summary: Estate agents say rising mortgage costs have created a mood of fear, with Canterbury among the cities being hit Have you lost a mortgage deal or seen your rate rise? On a warm, spring morning in Canterbury, the cobbled streets are buzzing with activity and the white Tudor houses gleam in the sun. It is a scene that seems far removed from events in the Middle East, but the conflict is undermining business and consumer confidence – rattling the city’s housing market just as the spring selling seas

stories.title: A festival of young European photography
stories.summary: The Circulation(s) photo festival has returned to Paris for its 16th edition. On show are works by 26 young European photographers. The projects capture the pulse of young European photography, its intuitions, challenges and commitments Circulation(s) will take place at the Centquatre-Paris from 21 March to 17 May 2026 Continue reading...

stories.title: Floods, power outages and hundreds evacuated as Cyclone Vaianu lashes New Zealand’s North Island
stories.summary: Cyclone crossed coast near Maketu peninsula, packing destructive winds exceeding 130km/h (80 mph), heavy rain and large swells Cyclone Vaianu made landfall in New Zealand’s North Island on Sunday, triggering floods, power outages and forcing hundreds to evacuate . The cyclone crossed the coast near the Maketu peninsula, packing destructive winds exceeding 130km/h (80 mph), heavy rain and large swells, national weather provider MetService said, describing Vaianu as a “life-threatening” system. Co

stories.title: Benin holds presidential election four months after failed coup
stories.summary: As president Patrice Talon steps down after a decade, the west African country’s finance minister is favourite to win This Sunday, just four months after a failed coup, Benin heads to the polls for a presidential election that feels more like a coronation than a contest. Patrice Talon, the businessman turned politician who has been president since 2016, is ineligible to run again after serving two five-year terms. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK starts crackdown on EU citizens’ post-Brexit rights
stories.summary: Concerns raised over use of travel data in determining if people are ‘continuously’ in Britain after HMRC fiasco UK ministers are to start removing post-Brexit residency rights from EU citizens who are no longer “continuously” living in the country. The initiative is legal under the 2020 Brexit withdrawal agreement, but the decision to use travel data to partly determine absences has raised concerns after the HMRC fiasco in which almost 20,000 parents were stripped of child benefits because of i

stories.title: Louisiana Republicans move to eliminate court office won by exonerated man
stories.summary: After Calvin Duncan served 28 years for a murder he didn’t commit, he won an election to serve as criminal court clerk. But now the office might be shut down A man imprisoned for nearly 30 years before being exonerated won a landmark election in New Orleans promising to fix a judicial system that failed him. Now, Louisiana’s governor, Jeff Landry, and the Republican-controlled state legislature are racing to eliminate his job before he can be sworn in. Calvin Duncan won 68% of the vote last Nove

stories.title: US state department revokes green cards of three Iranian nationals it links to regime
stories.summary: Three arrested by federal agents had family ties to Iranian military general, regime spokesperson or security chief United States federal agents arrested three Iranian nationals – including the son of a revolutionary at the center of the 1979 Iran hostage crisis – after the US state department terminated their green cards, the department announced on Saturday. State department officials revoked the green card status of Seyed Eissa Hashemi, whose mother was an Iranian revolutionary who served as

stories.title: Nigeria terror trial: Nearly 400 terror suspects convicted
stories.summary: Hundreds of Islamist militants went on trial with prosecutors securing 386 convictions. Nigeria is in the midst of an insurgency involving extremist militant groups, including Boko Haram.

stories.title: US and Iran have not reached an agreement, Vance says
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance says Iranian negotiators chose not to accept the US' terms, which Iran says were "unreasonable" and "excessive." DW has more.

stories.title: What could come after a rules-based world order?
stories.summary: The war between the US, Israel and Iran was, for many, a sign that the rules-based world order, a set of principles developed after World War II, is collapsing. If that era is over, what could be next?

stories.title: Ireland clears refinery blockade as fuel protests trigger nationwide shortages
stories.summary: Authorities used force reopen the country's only refinery after days of disruption over petrol prices left fuel pumps dry and threatened emergency services.

stories.title: UK freezes deal to return Chagos Islands to Mauritius
stories.summary: A fallout with President Trump has seen the planned handing over of the islands put on ice. At the center of the debate is the use of a joint UK-US military base.

stories.title: Armenia and Azerbaijan civil societies hold new round of broad talks to advance peace process
stories.summary: Armenian and Azerbaijan’s representatives met for the fourth round of talks as part of the so-called Peace Bridge Initiative to advance the historic peace process between the two South Caucasus neighbours.

stories.title: 'It's a special thing to be on Planet Earth': Artemis crew welcomed home in Houston
stories.summary: The four astronauts flew around the Moon in a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans in history.

stories.title: Iran war lands 'triple blow' to flood-ravaged Sri Lankans
stories.summary: The Iran war could not have come at a worse time for Sri Lanka, reeling from devastating floods and an economic crisis.

stories.title: The prophet and the mysterious death of Charmain Speirs

stories.summary: Easter festivities are muted in Kharkiv as Ukrainians expect fighting to flare up again after a weekend truce.

stories.summary: The high-profile Democrat, a leading contender in California's governor race, firmly denies the accusations.

stories.title: US and Iran end peace and ceasefire talks in Pakistan without agreement
stories.summary: The United States and Iran did not reach an agreement at the end of the historic talks in Pakistan, but the fact that the two sides engaged in face-to-face, direct talks after hours in which the Pakistani side was shuttling between them marked a certain breakthrough in negotiations.

stories.title: Surging gas prices drive US inflation to highest level in 2 years
stories.summary: Inflation in the U.S. shot up to its highest level in two years in March, and consumer sentiment is at a record low. As the effects of President Donald Trump’s war with Iran ripple across the economy, the combination of surging prices and grim attitudes is a warning sign to Republicans seeking to maintain control of Congress in the midterm elections. Oil and gas prices exploded in March as global energy markets absorbed a once-in-a-generation supply shock from the war with Iran. The halting of t

stories.title: Four men deported by US to Eswatini have right to see lawyer, court rules
stories.summary: The men, sent to the southern African country in July, have been denied in-person counsel for nine months Four men deported by the US to Eswatini and denied in-person legal counsel for nine months while detained in a maximum security prison have the right to see a local lawyer, Eswatini’s supreme court ruled. The men, from Cambodia, Cuba, Vietnam and Yemen, were sent to the small southern African country , formerly known as Swaziland, in July despite having no connection to the country, as part

stories.title: Ukraine : Russian drone attacks hit Sumy and Odesa as Easter truce looms
stories.summary: Russian drone strikes hit Sumy and Odesa, injuring two and damaging homes and energy sites, as Putin proposes a brief Easter ceasefire.

stories.title: Is Iran Trump’s Suez crisis, or just a passing thunderstorm?

stories.title: Albanese didn’t return with shiploads of diesel. That doesn’t mean his Singapore visit wasn’t a success

stories.title: US man in Bahamian jail after wife disappears into Atlantic waters during boat trip
stories.summary: Lynette and Brian Hooker, from Michigan, were years into a sailing adventure when Brian said his wife fell overboard Lynette Hooker bounced around the deck of the docked Soul Mate, smiled into the camera and proclaimed, “We’re finally leaving Kemah,” referring to a Texas port town. “It’s only been four months,” she said as her husband, Brian, tugged on some rigging as they got ready to set sail. stories.title: Trump says plans filed for huge triumphal arch in Washington
stories.summary: The US arch would be over 50% taller than the famous Arc de Triomphe in Paris, according to the official plans. The US president has described the project as "the GREATEST and MOST BEAUTIFUL Triumphal Arch" in the world.

stories.title: India news: Jaishankar meets UAE deputy PM to discuss Iran war
stories.summary: India is reaching out to its key economic partners in the Middle East to take stock of the impact of the Iran war and ensure smooth trade. Meanwhile, PM Modi is busy campaigning in West Bengal. DW has more.

stories.title: Ukraine: Attacks continue as Orthodox Easter ceasefire nears

stories.title: Is Iran unable to locate its mines in the Strait of Hormuz?

stories.summary: Judges handed down sentences ranging from five years to life imprisonment following mass trials.

stories.title: Peruvians to go to polls hoping to break cycle of instability

About 27 million people who are eligible to vote must choose between a record 35 presidential candidates as well as contenders for the bicameral congre

stories.title: Concerns raised about motorbike tourist trail after death of British teenager in Vietnam

stories.title: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he co-founded

stories.summary: Dobrev Klára nagyjából 250 ember előtt lépett színpadra a Baranya megyei faluban.

stories.title: Remény már alig, de mi még itt vagyunk
stories.summary: Az Örkény Színház Országkórusa különleges közösségi kísérlet. Nem hagyományos színházi előadás. Egy installációból, fikciós darabból és állampolgári performanszból álló egész estés program, amely arra keresi a választ: akarunk-e gyereket vállalni ebben az országban? Kritika.

stories.title: Vági János: Hallgatólagos beleegyezés
stories.summary: „Az apám a ruhamolyokat gyűlölte legjobban az élőlények közül, leszámítva az embereket.” E heti novellánk egy elnyomó apafigura bukásáról szól.

stories.title: Londonban nem lesz tiszás külképviseleti megfigyelő, de fideszes és mi hazánkos kettő is
stories.summary: A Fidesz 42 delegáltjából 39-et, a Tisza 71 jelöltjéből csak 29-et vett nyilvántartásba a választási bizottság, de a legtöbb fontos városban azért így is lesz ellenzéki megfigyelő. Az NVB elárulta, miért utasította el a delegáltakat, mi pedig megmutatjuk, melyik külképviseleteken lesznek jelen az egyes pártok.

stories.title: Trump a kilépés emlegetése nélkül is újabb szöget verhet a NATO koporsójába
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök fortyog, amiért az európai országok nem segítették az iráni háborúban, dühét pedig a napokban Mark Rutte NATO-főtitkárra zúdította. Rutte egyelőre betölti a villámhárító szerepét, de egyre nehezebb a dolga.

stories.title: A Mészáros Lőrinc érdekeltségéhez tartozó Femina szerint jólesik a vasárnapi ebéd után a pihe-puha narancsos bukta
stories.summary: A választások előtt két nappal osztottak meg a narancsos bukta receptjét, az időzítés és a narancsos töltelék mindenképpen ad egy kis érdekes felhangot.

stories.title: Vajdasági levélszavazás: több választó nem kapott borítékot, egy halott viszont igen
stories.summary: Vannak, akik simán odaadják egy idegennek a borítékolatlan voksukat, vagy nem értik, miért aggályos, ha aktivisták kézbesítenek, mások nem is tudják, hogy jogosultak a szavazásra, meg egyáltalán, hogy választás lesz.

stories.title: Kivezettek a rendőrök egy kormánypárti influenszert a Fidesz kampányzárójáról
stories.summary: A rendőrök összesen négy embert igazoltattak az esettel kapcsolatban.

stories.title: Recycling's next big thing — or big bluff?
stories.summary: An advanced recycling process claims it can handle hard-to-recycle plastics, like packaging. But critics say it's not worth the effort.

stories.title: Germany: Police suspect antisemitism in Israeli restaurant attack in Munich
stories.summary: Police and restaurant owners believe antisemitism motivated the Munich attack. Nobody was hurt, but three windows were broken, causing damage worth several thousand euros. This comes amid tension over the war in Iran.

stories.title: NASA's billion-dollar space race goes into overdrive
stories.summary: NASA's renewed push to get a human mission to the moon and Mars is neither cheap, nor for the faint of heart. But who ends up covering the costs and what does everyone still on Earth get out of all this?

stories.title: First gay rights movement: Berlin's wild 1920s queer history
stories.summary: Before the Nazis took power, Berlin was a center of LGBTQ+ rights and queer culture, with research institutes, a flourishing nightlife and one of the earliest gay rights' movements.

stories.title: Putin announces Easter ceasefire, proposed by Kyiv for weeks
stories.summary: Moscow repeatedly ignored Kyiv’s calls for Easter weekend ceasefire around 12 April just to announce what it called its own “unilateral" truce.

stories.title: Pakistan prepares to host US-Iran talks as ceasefire remains shaky
stories.summary: Islamabad is getting ready to host talks on Friday between the United States and Iran on a resolution to the war and the Strait of Hormuz, which remains at a standstill despite the ceasefire.

stories.title: Pentagon denies threatening Vatican over pope's remarks on US intervention in Iran
stories.summary: Pentagon and Vatican envoys have both denied reports that US officials gave the Holy See's ambassador to the US a "bitter lecture," demanding the Church align with the Trump administration over Pope Leo XIV's criticism of the Iran war.

stories.title: Overlooked IRGC institution holds key to Iran's Hormuz approach, expert warns
stories.summary: The fragile US-Iran ceasefire is already faltering, with Tehran still blocking the Strait of Hormuz navigation in protest against Israel’s attacks in Lebanon. A leading Iran expert indicates Tehran's Hormuz strategy could be part of a well-established strategy.

stories.title: Israeli restaurant targeted in suspected antisemitic attack in Munich
stories.summary: Antisemitic offences in Germany have risen significantly in recent years, reaching a new peak in 2024. Authorities recorded 6,236 cases, including 173 violent incidents, according to available data.

stories.title: Mr Nobody on leaving Russia behind: Oscar-winning director Pavel Talankin talks home truths
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview, Paval Talankin, winner of the 2026 Best Documentary Oscar, tells Euronews about live outside his Russian homeland and the "discarded" generation for whom he made 'Mr Nobody Against Putin'.

stories.title: Ongoing wars 'not failure of UN but of member states', General Assembly President tells Euronews
stories.summary: The head of the United Nations' main policy-making organ responded to mounting criticism of her organisation in a Euronews interview, arguing the abuse of vetoes by key members is blocking progress.

stories.title: Former hostage In Iran faces surreal tax troubles in France after release
stories.summary: Former French hostage in Iran, Benjamin Brière, said his release did not end his ordeal, as he struggled with taxes, benefits and reintegration back home.

stories.title: Emmanuel Macron meets Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican to discuss global tensions
stories.summary: France’s President Emmanuel Macron met Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican on Friday, marking their first official talks and signalling renewed dialogue between France and the Holy See at a time of rising global tensions.

stories.title: Israel: Hezbollah rocket from Lebanon strikes Safed, injuring several people
stories.summary: Emergency services were deployed in Safed on 10 April 2026 after a rocket fired from Lebanon by Hezbollah struck the northern city, prompting a rapid response to treat casualties and secure the area.

stories.title: Italy: From Go to Monopoly, board games bridge generations
stories.summary: A new exhibition titled “0–99. Design for Play” opened on 10 April 2026 at Palazzo Arese Borromeo in Cesano Maderno, near Milan, turning a 17th‑century aristocratic residence into a large, interactive gallery of games.

stories.title: JD Vance warns Iran not to 'play' US during planned talks in Pakistan
stories.summary: The US vice president said he was "looking forward to negotiations" in Pakistan as UK Prime Minister Starmer classified the ceasefire as "fragile".

stories.title: Artemis II mission was a triumph - now comes the hard part
stories.summary: The mission was almost flawless but there are considerable obstacles ahead before a Moon landing.

stories.title: This idyllic US town was full of police families - and a serial killer in their midst
stories.summary: For years, rumours swirled about who could be behind the bodies being discovered on the shores of Gilgo Beach.

stories.title: Concerns after satellite provider restricts Iran images following US pressure
stories.summary: Planet Labs says it is restricting coverage of Iran and parts of the Middle East indefinitely.

stories.title: NASA's Artemis II astronauts back on earth after record-breaking trip around the moon
stories.summary: With their flight now complete, the four astronauts have set NASA up for a moon landing by another crew in just two years and a full-blown moon base within the decade.

stories.title: A day in the life of a 19-year-old in ICE detention: ‘I feel that this nightmare is not going to end’
stories.summary: Olivia has been detained for months at the sprawling Dilley center in Texas. She has lost 20lb, and wakes up every day with a headache Each day in detention feels like 48 hours for Olivia. The 19-year-old asylum seeker from the Democratic Republic of the Congo has been at the Dilley Immigration processing center in Texas for more than four months. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ukraine and Syria tighten relations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's visit to Syria was not only a diplomatic gesture but motivated by strategic considerations regarding Russia, once a strong supporter of the Middle Eastern state.

stories.title: US accused of pressuring Latin America to cut ties with Cuban doctors program
stories.summary: Cuba accuses US of ‘extorting’ countries in pushing them to axe deals with Havana to send doctors on medical missions Cuba’s foreign minister has accused the United States of “extorting” Latin American countries by putting pressure on them to cancel decades-old deals with Havana for the supply of doctors. Bruno Rodríguez said the United States was trying to “strangle” the economy of the communist island, which earns billions from its foreign medical missions, after several countries stopped depl

stories.title: Wild chimpanzees recorded waging ‘civil war’ with coordinated attacks between two groups
stories.summary: New study describes what may be the first case of a unified community of chimps, in Uganda, turning on itself On a June day in 2015, primatologist Aaron Sandel was quietly observing a small cluster of the Ngogo chimpanzee group in Uganda’s Kibale national park when he noticed something strange. As other members of the chimpanzees’ wider group moved closer through the forest, the chimpanzees in front of him began to display nervous behaviour. They grimaced and touched each other for reassurance,

stories.title: Islamabad prepares to host historic negotiations between Iran and the US
stories.summary: In Pakistan’s capital, the army has been deployed, a public holiday has been declared and the streets are eerily empty Middle East crisis – live updates The streets of Islamabad were on strict lockdown as Pakistan’s capital prepared to play host to historic negotiations between Iran and the US that have dangled the promise of an end to war that has devastated the Middle East. Even as the US-Iran ceasefire looked increasingly precarious , amid Israel’s continued bombardment of Lebanon and dispute

stories.title: Artemis II crew emotional as mission prepares for reentry
stories.summary: In less than 48 hours, the Orion spacecraft is due to splash down in the Pacific Ocean. Crew members have said what they saw in space will forever change how they see the world.

stories.title: Veteran German actor Mario Adorf dies aged 95
stories.summary: Few actors had a career as rich as Mario Adorf. He played legendary Hollywood villains, wrote German TV history and still had film roles at age 90.

stories.title: Migrant dinghy sinks in English Channel off France, 4 people die
stories.summary: A boat carrying more than 40 people ran into trouble early in the morning, with two men and two women dying in the waters. Crossings are building up after the winter lull, but the sea temperatures are slower to rise.

stories.title: Ukraine: Russia's Putin declares Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: Russian President Putin has announced a ceasefire in its war in Ukraine over the Orthodox Easter holiday. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy said his country will "act accordingly" during the truce.

stories.title: Lufthansa cabin crew begin strike
stories.summary: Hundreds of flights have been canceled as cabin crew members walk out. This is Lufthansa's third major strike this year.

stories.title: Iran rules out any restrictions on its enrichment programme, nuclear chief says
stories.summary: The issue of uranium enrichment has been central to Western relations with Iran for more than two decades, with the US and its allies accusing Tehran of seeking atomic weapons.

stories.title: UAE urges full compliance from Iran following ceasefire announcement
stories.summary: The UAE is seeking clarifications on the US-Iran ceasefire deal regarding attacks across the Gulf, as well as on the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz as a “central priority”, as Tehran's blockade continues despite its commitment to reopen the strait to free navigation.

stories.title: Trump again berates NATO, calls it 'disappointing'
stories.summary: Trump's outrage at NATO allies over their failure to join the Iran war had prompted fears he would seek to pull the United States out of the nearly eight-decade-old alliance.

stories.title: Germany has resumed diplomatic talks with Iran, Chancellor Friedrich Merz says
stories.summary: Merz also said that the continued Israeli military campaign in Lebanon could jeopardise peace talks expected between the United States and Iran over the war in the Middle East.
